## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hey guys, in this video we're going to be talking about Upwork Verification Badge. So this is the badge that displays on your profile and lets business owners that are looking for freelancers know that your identity has been verified, you're a real legitimate person, right? You're a real person, you're not a robot, and you're very likely not a scammer, right? So it just builds a lot of trust. So we're going to go through what that looks like, how you can get that done, and just what the whole process means because it's a little odd and it's a little strange when you first go through and first experience it. So on the top of your profile, if you haven't verified already, you will eventually at some point see this badge just all, actually not on the top of your profile across your entire Upwork, wherever you're on Upwork you'll see this badge displayed on the banner displayed on the top. I actually got mine last verified in January and the process was a bit odd. This popped up, I believe I had already had it verified and then this popped up and I was like okay and I verified it and I was super confused talking to the support because I was like I was already verified and then they told me I had to get on a video call and I was like alright and I got on a video call and I literally had to, there was no one else actually there, like they didn't have their webcam on, it was just me, my webcam and I had to show my face and I had to show my passport and hold it up so they could take a photo so they could verify my face to the face on the passport and take a photo of that. So it was a really odd situation and I was just like really thrown off by it and so I just want to throw that out there that you will at some point have to go through a video verification process to get your identity verified or to maintain that identity verification. So again that's how it's going to look when they prompt you to do it and then on your profile when you don't have it on it's going to kind of look like this, this is actually a freelancer that I am considering working with but yeah with WordPress development. Anyway, so he does not have it here if you can see my mouse, he doesn't have it next to his name so his identity is not verified. However, this is another freelancer I've worked with and her identity is verified, you can see that right here and you can see that little badge that means it is verified but I do want to caution that as important it is to get verified because it's a trust signal and it also obviously you know it helps algorithm. It's just something that Upwork likes right and they like people to have their identity verified to be trustworthy. I do want to say that it's not something that is required and I'll prove that right now. So this is a profile of a CPA and as you can see his hourly rate, his income earned, he's earned over half a million on Upwork as a platform and an incredible amount of time on Upwork, 89% job success score, very interesting for 506 jobs.

## [03:04 - 04:19]

He does not have his verification, his Upwork profile verified with his identity. Maybe it's falling off, I don't remember last time I looked at his profile whether it was on there or not but all I'm saying is it's not there right now. So it's clearly not something you need and I don't think he's going to struggle to get jobs considering the size of his profile but if you're just starting out, you don't have Upwork history, why not get verified? It's just going to help you in the algorithm, it's just going to help you with potential clients building more trust so it's a really important thing to get done that's only going to take a couple minutes. I also did have to re-go through the Upwork verification process last night ironically and it prompted me again and I was again very confused because I was like, hey, I got verified in January, why am I having to do this again? I had to do another video call last night. He didn't actually make me hold up my passport which is cool, I guess we wanted to see my face. So it was a little odd but I'm assuming that's something that's probably going to happen every six months or so. Just something to keep in mind that every once in a while you have to re-verify and everything's going to have to match up with your profile, your name and address and all of that. So yeah, definitely get verified because it's super quick and it's going to help.
